meta.title: Part 5 — human-authored benchmark (30 items)
meta.notes: Handwritten for quality comparison with Ollama; verify keys before study.

items.prompt: Unless the invoice ------- by noon, we cannot authorize the release of inventory.
items.explanation: After unless, present tense can express future condition; will be paid misreads unless as a main clause promise.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense item checks the verb inside the unless clause when the matrix clause uses future-related limitation on the warehouse release tied to payment timing.
The opening signal is "Unless the invoice" plus the gap—here the subordinate clause must state the condition under which release stays blocked, not a future promise about paying later.
A strong test-taker might pick B will be paid because future payment sounds natural, but that turns the unless clause into an odd sequence rather than a single present condition for authorization.

Option B adds unnecessary future auxiliary inside the condition; option C has been paid is aspectually heavy where a simple state of payment by the deadline matters; both clash with the unless-time frame beside noon.

The correct wording is is paid because it states the condition plainly; contrast with option B where will be paid shifts the grammar away from the standard unless + present pattern for stipulated deadlines.

items.prompt: The procurement director asked vendors to ------- all bids electronically before Friday at 5 p.m.
items.explanation: After to the bare infinitive submit is required with implied subject vendors.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item tests the form after to when vendors is the logical subject of the infinitive inside the reported request about electronic bids.
Focus near the gap: "asked vendors to" shows an infinitive slot where the same lexical verb base should follow without an agreement suffix tied to director.
People sometimes choose submitting because -ing feels active, but asked already sets to + base verb for the requested action list.
Letter C submitting keeps -ing where the structure expects base submit after to; letter D submission is a noun where a verb phrase is required after to.
Choose submit as the exact string after to; compare with submission which cannot act as the bare action verb form required in this director instruction.

items.prompt: Due to ------- delays along the coastal route, the delivery window will shift to next Tuesday.
items.explanation: Due to + noun phrase needs an adjective modifying delays; persistently is adverb; persistence and persist are wrong category.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form question controls how due to leads into a nominal phrase naming the delays affecting the coastal delivery estimate.
The fragment "Due to" expects a modifier for delays — quote the phrase with delays to anchor the decision on the head noun rather than a free-floating adverb.
Readers may want persistently because it emphasizes ongoing trouble, but it cannot directly modify delays without awkward stacking compared with adjective persistent.
Option B persistence is a noun that would force a different phrase shape; option D persist is a verb form that cannot sit where an attributive modifier before delays is needed.
persistent fits as the exact adjective spelling before delays; contrast persist which is verbal and cannot occupy that attributive slot here.

items.prompt: The contract stipulates that either party may terminate the agreement ------- thirty days written notice.
items.explanation: Standard collocation: terminate with notice (instrument/means), not by thirty days.
analysisSteps: This vocabulary item tests the preposition packaging termination rights with the formal channel described thirty days written notice.
The legal tone appears around "terminate the agreement" where business contracts usually specify notice mechanics rather than calendar endpoints alone.
Some examinees grab after notice imagining sequence, but the sentence needs how termination happens in relation to notice, not a later vague moment.
Option B by often pairs with agent phrases, not this notice packaging; option C after suggests post-notice instant termination without the required parallel structure here.
with carries the sense of accompanying notice as required; compare with after which fails the contract register for paired notice instruments in policy drafts.

items.prompt: Management will not ------- the budget increase unless the risk committee signs off first.
items.explanation: Modal will + bare infinitive: sign off as verb phrase without -ing or past.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form problem tests what may follow the negative modal will not before an idiomatic phrasal chunk about budgets.
Quote structure around "Management will not" to see that the gap needs the base verb cluster that completes the predicate without an extra finite marker.
Test-takers may select signing off on because the progressive feels emphatic, yet will not already needs a plain infinitive complement.
Option B signing off on supplies an -ing where a base form is mandatory; option C signed off on reads like an inappropriate tense chunk after will not.
sign off on is the exact multiword string needed; contrast signing off on which keeps the wrong nonfinite shape for this modal frame.

items.prompt: The team finalized the slide deck, ------- that every chart referenced audited figures.
items.explanation: Participial clause explaining result: ensuring, parallel to the main clause subject team.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item examines how the sentence adds a result clause after finalizing the slide deck about audited figures.
Anchor on "finalized the slide deck" — the following modifier should show concurrent outcome control without introducing another full finite verb for team again.
People often pick to ensure thinking purpose, but a comma-style elaboration here favors a participial lead ensuring that explains the quality check.
Option B to ensure can work in some decks but creates a different structural hook; option C ensures would need a subject and clashes with punctuation.
ensuring is the exact -ing form matching this supplement; compare ensure which would be bare and cannot open this clause after the comma before the gap.

items.prompt: Client feedback suggests that the onboarding portal should be more ------- for first-time applicants.
items.explanation: Comparative more + adjective intuitive; intuition is noun; intuitively adverb misplaces form.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form question asks for the adjective after comparative more when describing an onboarding portal quality for first-time applicants.
Pull wording from "more" plus gap — the slot must be gradable adjective, not an adverb describing how HR staff acts.
Learners sometimes choose intuitively because adverbs feel vivid, but more already expects an adjective head for the portal itself.
Option B intuitively is adverb sized wrong for the predicate adjective slot; option C intuition is a noun where adjective sense is mandatory after more.
intuitive is the precise adjective spelling; contrast intuition which is nominal and cannot follow more in this comparative frame.

items.prompt: The merger is subject to ------- regulatory approval in both jurisdictions before any funds may transfer.
items.explanation: Subject to + adjective + noun: final regulatory approval.
analysisSteps: This item targets the pattern after subject to when naming the regulatory hurdle before cross-border fund movement.
The fixed frame "subject to" introduces the gap before "regulatory approval" so you need an adjective that can stack naturally with that compound approval noun in both jurisdictions.
Some pick finally because it sounds emphatic, but finally is an adverb and cannot premodify regulatory approval here.
Option C finalize is a verb where an adjective slot precedes the compound noun; option D finalized is past participle adjective implying completed state prematurely.
final is the exact adjective string; compare finalize which is the verb form not fitting between subject to and regulatory approval.

items.prompt: ------- the quarterly targets were missed, leadership still expanded the internship cohort.
items.explanation: Subordinate clause with finite verb needs subordinator Although; Despite needs noun phrase.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Connector problem links a concessive fact about missed quarterly targets to a surprising expansion of internships inside leadership decisions.
The opening gap precedes a full clause "the quarterly targets were missed" requiring a subordinator rather than a preposition-noun bundle alone.
Many choose Despite because contrast feels right, but despite must be followed by a noun or gerund chunk, not a subject-verb clause starting the way shown.
Option B Despite cannot head a finite clause opener; option C However is adverbial and would need punctuation patterns unlike although leading directly into a clause.
Although is the exact connector string; contrast Despite which cannot introduce this finite clause after the gap position at sentence front.

items.prompt: The facility upgrade is scheduled to conclude ------- the end of the fiscal year, absent further supply shocks.
items.explanation: by the end = deadline completion; until focuses on duration through endpoint.
analysisSteps: This vocabulary timing item sets a completion boundary for the facility upgrade relative to the fiscal calendar and supply risk.
The phrase "the end of the fiscal year" pairs with deadlines where finish-by semantics differ from stretch during semantics.
People may pick until imagining the work continuing across the year, yet the upgrade is slated to finish rather than run up continuously through that phrase alone.
Option B until suggests ongoing action through the endpoint without the natural completion-at-deadline nuance; option C since anchors a start, not a finish.
by states completion no later than that endpoint; compare with until which misreads the scheduler language around conclude for this upgrade window.

items.prompt: The sales lead attributed the shortfall ------- delayed shipments from the overseas plant.
items.explanation: Attribute X to Y is fixed collocation.
analysisSteps: This vocabulary collocations test pairs the reporting verb attributed with the right preposition introducing the overseas shipment cause.
Notice "attributed the shortfall" — standard business writing threads an explanation object with a cause phrase afterward.
Readers occasionally insert for like blame for, yet attribute selects to when naming the source factor in formal review memos.
Option B for follows verbs like apologize but not attribute in this pattern; option C with suggests accompaniment, not cause mapping from shortfall to shipments.
to is the exact preposition expected; contrast for which breaks the attribute … to construction about delayed shipments from the plant.

items.prompt: Compliance requires that every contractor ------- a site-safety briefing before badge access.
items.explanation: Subjunctive or base form after require that in formal US English: every contractor complete.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense item addresses the verb form after require that when the noun every contractor heads the clause about badge access rules.
The structure "requires that every contractor" sets up a mandative style clause where the bare base verb often appears in formal safety policies.
Some pick completes because third-person singular seems grammatical, yet the legal register frequently keeps the base form here instead of an -s form.
Option B completes adds -s where mandative base compete patterns appear; option C completed is wrongly past inside an ongoing policy requirement.
complete is the exact base string chosen; contrast completes which misapplies regular agreement where the mandative register favors the unmarked form.

items.prompt: The CFO emphasized accuracy ------- speed when reviewing the restructuring figures.
items.explanation: Accuracy over speed = priority preposition in comparative preference.
analysisSteps: This vocabulary item contrasts two abstract performance goals CFOs weigh while scanning restructuring spreadsheets for errors.
Read "emphasized accuracy" alongside the rival noun speed in the parallel noun structure needing a priority preposition rather than comparison of inequality alone.
People may try than linking comparative adjectives, but accuracy and speed are nouns here needing preference language not comparativethan scaffolding.
Option B than expects adjective comparatives elsewhere; option C against signals opposition scenarios unlike simple priority ordering inside emphasis.
over communicates ranking accuracy ahead of speed; compare than which fails to tie the two nouns in the intended CFO emphasis formula.

items.prompt: The HR memo reminds employees to ------- confidential data on shared drives only when encryption is verified.
items.explanation: Reminds … to + base verb store before object confidential data.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item sets the shape after reminds employees to regarding encryption-checked handling of confidential data folders.
Quote "reminds employees to" which primes an infinitive without an extra suffix tied to memo reminding itself.
Some learners choose storing because gerunds appear after to in other idioms, but after reminds someone to the bare verb is standard HR wording.
Option B stored is passive/adjective wrong for active employee action; option C storage is a noun where verb action about drives is required after to.
store is the exact verb string required; contrast storing which mistakenly supplies an -ing where the company reminder expects the base action form.

items.prompt: The marketing team will roll ------- the pilot campaign once the legal disclaimer is finalized.
items.explanation: Phrasal verb roll out (launch) matches campaign; roll on is idiomatically different.
analysisSteps: This vocabulary item targets the phrasal chunk with roll when discussing a pilot campaign gated by a legal disclaimer approval.
The verb cluster "will roll" plus the gap must pair with a particle that matches pilot campaign launch language around finalized disclaimers.
Candidates sometimes select roll on assuming progress, but roll on is not the marketing launch idiom paired with campaign here.
Option B on fails the standard product launch idiom tested; option C over involves review motions unlike issuing the campaign externally.
out completes roll out meaning launch; compare roll on which does not capture the go-live nuance marketing intends after disclaimer sign-off.

items.prompt: Having ------- the vendor roster, the committee narrowed the shortlist to three qualified firms.
items.explanation: Perfect participle Having + past participle reviewed opens the sentence; review/reviewing wrong.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense problem uses a perfect participle opener before the committee action on vendor qualification lists.
The lead-in "Having" must pair with a past participle describing a completed survey step before narrowing shortlists.
Some pick reviewing because -ing follows having in casual speech, but formal Having reviewed is the standard academese bundle.
Option B reviewing repeats -ing in a way that collides with Having; option C reviews is finite verb material incompatible after Having alone.
reviewed is the exact past participle string; contrast review which is the bare form inappropriate directly after Having in this reduced clause.

items.prompt: The IT notice stated that remote access credentials ------- every ninety days for contract workers.
items.explanation: Plural subject credentials takes plural verb expire in formal agreement.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense sentence checks subject-verb alignment where remote access credentials headline a policy sentence about ninety-day cycles.
Pull subject details from "credentials" plural near the gap so finite verb choice hinges on plural agreement not singular expires.
Some test-takers lean expires because IT topics often use singular mass nouns, but credentials here is plural requiring plural verb shape.
Option B expires pairs singular verb with plural credentials inappropriately; option C expired would be oddly past for a standing policy statement.
expire is plural verb form matching credentials; contrast expires which wrongly singularizes the verb beside plural credentials here.

items.prompt: All participants must ------- non-disclosure agreements before viewing prototype schematics.
items.explanation: Modal must + bare infinitive sign before object NDA.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item locks the verb form after the deontic modal must for NDA handling prior to prototype schematics access.
Quote "must" in the matrix clause controlling required behavior before viewing confidential drawings at the session.
Learners sometimes pick signing after must due to memorized patterns, yet must still selects base infinitive sign for this directive.
Option B signing is progressive where must demands bare sign; option C signed would need different auxiliary support outside this modal frame.
sign is the exact infinitive string; compare signing which keeps the wrong -ing shape after must in formal lab protocols.

items.prompt: The logistics dashboard flags any shipment that ------- idle at a hub for more than forty-eight hours.
items.explanation: Relative clause subject that refers back to shipment (singular); remains agrees.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense item tests agreement inside a relative clause where the antecedent shipment controls the verb beside idle at hubs.
Focus on "any shipment that" where that recovers singular shipment requiring singular verb form before idle durations.
People may choose remain imagining proximity to any as plural, yet the true subject for the verb is the singular shipment head noun.
Option B remain would pluralize wrongly relative to shipment; option C remained shifts tense without a discourse reason for simple present monitoring.
remains matches singular shipment referenced by that; contrast remain which pluralizes incorrectly in this dashboard alert sentence.

items.prompt: To expedite reimbursement, employees should ------- itemized receipts alongside the travel claim form.
items.explanation: Modal should + base attach; attaches wrong for plural implied subject employees.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form prompt governs the verb after should when telling employees how to pair receipts with travel claims.
The lead purpose phrase "To expedite reimbursement" frames procedural guidance where should takes bare infinitive next.
Some choose attaching believing parallel with preceding verbs, but should still commands base attach before object itemized receipts.
Option B attaches adds third-person marking where plural employees with modal should still expect plain attach; option C attached is past where instruction is present.
attach is the precise base form; contrast attaches which misapplies singular agreement after should in policy prose.

items.prompt: The analyst compared monthly revenue ------- year-over-year growth benchmarks rather than quarter snapshots alone.
items.explanation: Compare X against Y is standard; compare for/about weaker here.
analysisSteps: This vocabulary item completes compared when juxtaposing monthly revenue with year-over-year growth benchmarks inside analyst commentary.
The predicate "compared monthly revenue" needs a following preposition before benchmarks that shows benchmark contrast rather than loose topic talk.
Some writers select about because discussion feels conversational, yet compared about is not the analytic preposition used with benchmarks here.
Option B for steers toward purpose more than juxtaposition; option C into suggests entry motion unlike benchmark laydowns.
against places revenue versus benchmarks cleanly; compare for which misses the comparative tension the analyst intends.

items.prompt: The warranty covers manufacturing defects but excludes damage ------- misuse or improper installation.
items.explanation: Due to + noun phrase misuse; because would need clause; owing needs to.
analysisSteps: This item supplies a cause phrase after excludes damage in a warranty scope sentence about improper handling categories.
Right after "excludes damage" the gap must introduce a nominal cause for damage such as misuse matching due to misuse, not a finite because clause without a subject-verb core.
Some pick because expecting cause, but because expects a subject-verb clause after it rather than noun misuse alone.
Option B because cannot sit before mere noun misuse without a verb; option C owing without to is incomplete compared with due to misuse.
due to misuse is the exact formula; contrast because which cannot introduce the noun-only complement string in this warranty carve-out.

items.prompt: Neither the project lead nor the two engineers ------- able to confirm the revised milestone dates on the call.
items.explanation: Neither nor with nearest plural subject engineers → plural verb were (formal agree with nor-phrase).
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense problem handles neither nor agreement where multiple noun types surround able to confirm milestone dates.
Important wording includes "nor the two engineers" suggesting plural human subjects closest to the verb phrase slot in formal usage variants.
People may choose was aligning with project lead singular, yet proximity patterns often pick plural were with plural engineers members in company usage tests.
Option B was conflicts with plural engineers presence; option C are might ignore past narrative frame if the call was yesterday in context.
were matches plural engineer group emphasis; contrast was which wrongly singularizes against engineers plurality nearest the gap.

items.prompt: The customer success team aims to ------- first-response times without sacrificing resolution quality.
items.explanation: Aims to + shorten (verb); short is adjective; shortens wrong after to.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form question sets the verb form after aims to when balancing faster first-response times against resolution depth.
Quote "aims to" showing infinitive destination where the root verb describing time adjustment should follow unchanged.
Learners pick short thinking adjective is enough, but to expects the verbal shorten targeting times metric rather than adjective short alone.
Option B shortens marks singular agreement where to needs plain shorten; option C shortening is gerund misaligned after aims to.
shorten is the exact infinitive verb required; compare short which is merely adjectival and cannot serve the verb slot after to alone.

items.prompt: If the prototype passes stress tests, the product team will ------- the launch by one quarter.
items.explanation: Future will + base delay; no -s on delay after will.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense clause links a conditional pass on stress tests to a scheduling verb about pushing the launch quarter boundary.
Key phrase "will" ahead of the gap shows a simple future auxiliary requiring base delay without extra agreement suffixes on delay itself.
Examinees sometimes select delays pairing with team as subject, but will already blocks third-person -s on the main verb delay.
Option B delayed is simple past incompatible with will future stack; option C delaying clashes unless progressive meaning intended, which it is not here.
delay is the base string after will; contrast delays which incorrectly adds agreement on delay after the auxiliary will here.

items.prompt: The security audit found that legacy passwords did not meet ------- complexity standards mandated last year.
items.explanation: Adjective minimum before complexity standards (attributive); minimal overlaps but minimum standard is fixed collocation.
analysisSteps: This vocabulary item selects an attributive adjective before complexity standards when describing outdated passwords in an audit memo.
The noun phrase "complexity standards" follows did not meet where auditors expect a regulatory-floor adjective naming the bar legacy passwords failed.
Some pick minimal because it feels similar, yet minimum complexity standards is a tighter collocation for baseline thresholds in IT policy prose.
Option B minimal is near-synonym stylistically weaker for threshold floors; option C minimally is adverb unable to modify standards noun directly.
minimum matches the standard phrase minimum complexity standards; compare minimize which is a verb and cannot occupy the adjective slot.

items.prompt: Upon ------- the revised contract, the director authorized counsel to archive the expired drafts immediately.
items.explanation: Upon + gerund receiving; Upon receive ungrammatical; Upon reception of would differ.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item needs the noun substitute after upon when the director acknowledges contract receipt before archival steps.
Opening with "Upon" forces either a gerund like receiving the revised contract or a reception noun phrase, not a bare verb stem directly after upon.
People may pick receive imagining base verb, but upon must take gerund receiving or noun object forms, not bare infinitive.
Option B receive is bare verb illegal right after upon; option C received would imply passive adjective wrongly strung without having clause.
receiving satisfies upon + gerund; contrast receive which cannot stand immediately after upon in this authorizing sentence.

items.prompt: The training session has been ------- until further notice because of facility maintenance delays.
items.explanation: Present perfect passive has been postponed; postpone base wrong; postponement needs different frame.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense sentence completes the passive present perfect when HR announces session shifts linked to maintenance timelines.
Phrase "has been" already opens passive voice so the gap must accept a past participle describing the training session state.
Readers may select postpone forgetting passive participle needed after has been for English voice patterns on session nouns.
Option B postpone is bare infinitive/participle confused after has been; option C postponing is progressive incompatible as main complement after has been here.
postponed is the exact past participle after has been; compare postpone which fails passive participle selection in this maintenance notice.

items.prompt: The sustainability report highlights a ------- reduction in single-use plastics across cafeterias this fiscal year.
items.explanation: Adjective measurable before reduction; measurably is adverb; measure is verb.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form prompt needs an adjective before reduction when summarizing quantifiable ESG cafeteria results for investors.
Structure "a" plus gap plus reduction signals attributive adjective slot describing how large the drop appears in data charts.
Some choose measurably because statistics feel numeric, yet the head noun reduction wants an adjective measurable not adverb measurably before it.
Option B measure is a verb or noun where adjective measurable is required; option D measures verb clashes with the determiner a before adjective zone.
measurable fits the attributive slot; compare measure which cannot modify reduction directly with the right part of speech.

items.prompt: The board will revisit the dividend policy ------- macroeconomic indicators stabilize in the target range.
items.explanation: Once + clause = after condition; during needs NP not clause; while odd for one-time stabilization.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Connector item sets a temporal gate linking board review of dividend policy to macroeconomic stabilization milestones.
Read "revisit the dividend policy" as the later main clause action depending on a subordinate trigger about indicators stabilizing.
Some pick while hoping for simultaneous backdrop, yet the sentence means the board waits until stabilization occurs rather than endless parallel time.
Option B while can mark simultaneous action but misfires for clear trigger-after condition style; option C during cannot head a finite clause starting macroeconomic indicators.
once marks the idea when indicators stabilize the board will act; compare during which cannot introduce this full clause about macroeconomic indicators after the gap.
